verdict.answer_first: Both answer immediately; A is cleaner: "EADDRINUSE means the port is already bound" while B says "a socket is already bound".
verdict.decisiveness: A commits faster to the core recommendation: "run... lsof"; B surveys more cases and over-weights PM2/launchd.
verdict.fabrication: Both invent environment details; A: "you run a PM2/launchd bridge"; B: "on your machine specifically — you run Node under a PM2/launchd bridge".
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives next-step branches: "If lsof shows a PID that comes back..." and covers Unix socket paths.
